steps.title: Choose a Protected Spot Outside
steps.description: Place the sensor on the exterior of your home, facing the direction of the most persistent source of noise, with a power source nearby. Pick a location that is not likely to get knocked or be especially exposed to rain, snow, or wind; a window or covered patio is generally a good choice.

steps.title: Secure the Sensor
steps.description: Attach the sensor using screws, cable ties, or foam tape, with the cable connector facing down. Make sure the mic whole remains unobstructed.

steps.title: Plan and Route the Cable
steps.description: Decide how you will run the cable from the power outlet to the device. Plan to run it along edges, corners, or walls where it will be less visible and not in the way. Measure how long a cable you will need, then add a little extra. Common USB cable lengths are 3, 6, 10, 20, and 30 feet; USB cables longer than 30 feet are not recommended.

steps.title: Install the Cable and Power On
steps.description: Install the cable and plug in the device. Use cable ties and mounts to secure the cable in place, then plug the device into a USB charger.

steps.title: Connect to the Setup Wi‑Fi
steps.description: Once the sensor is plugged in, use your computer or phone to connect to the sensor’s Wi‑Fi network called “tRacket_Setup” using the password “noise123”. In your browser, you will see a form to fill in your own Wi‑Fi login info and email address. If needed, select “Sign into network” for the browser to open. Be sure to enter your exact Wi‑Fi network name and password.

steps.title: Finish Setup and Log In
steps.description: The sensor will try to connect to the Wi‑Fi and internet. If it is unsuccessful, it will ask you to try again. Once successful, you will receive an email confirming that the sensor is online with a link to log in to the sensor manager. You can now log in to the sensor manager to specify the sensor location.
